title: 山茱萸花
description: 由山茱萸树上的花朵重新创造而成的瓷器，有四个粉红色的花瓣（实际上是外层叶子，称为苞片），围绕着一个巨大的微型花球，由数百个绿白色的小穗状花序组成，上面有棕色的花球；苞片后面是四片棕色的外层叶子，用金色和铂金装饰，以模拟干燥的叶子，还有一根颜色相同的粗茎。这座雕塑像一个三脚架一样站在两片棕色的叶子和茎上，所以花面向侧面，略微向上。Sugiura Yasuyoshi自2010年以来一直在制作花卉雕塑。大多数是花朵的相对字面放大，经过仔细观察，然后在瓷器中仔细再现。他的一些花在盛开的高峰期被摘下，具有很高的装饰性。其他则被描绘成干涸和/或变成种子。他还创作了花卉装置。他在日本最为人所知的是他早期的作品，这些作品由石头的陶瓷铸件组成，排列或堆叠成大型装置。
description_original_en: Porcelain re-creation of the flower from a kousa dogwood tree, with four pink-tipped petals (actually outer leaves, called bracts), surrounding a large central ball of miniature flowers, consisting of hundreds of small greenish-white spikes topped by brown balls; behind the bracts are four brownish outer leaves finished in gold and platinum to simulate dried foliage, and a thick stem of the same color.  The sculpture stands as a tripod on two of the brown leaves and the stem so the flower faces sideways and slightly upward.   Sugiura Yasuyoshi has made flower sculptures since 2010.  Most are relatively literal enlargements of flowers, closely observed and then carefully recreated in porcelain. Some of his flowers are caught at the peak of their bloom and are highly decorative.  Others are depicted dried and/or gone to seed. He has also created installations of flowers.  He is best known in Japan for his earlier work, which consisted of ceramic casts of stones, arranged or piled into large installations.

title: 方字书法：穿越布鲁克林码头，沃尔特·惠特曼
description: 这幅画由两块画板（a、b）组成。沃尔特·惠特曼（Walt Whitman）的《穿越布鲁克林渡口》（Crossing Brooklyn Ferry）的第一首诗是用“方字书法”写成的，这是艺术家发明的混合书写形式。这种书写系统由英语单词组成，字母排列成类似汉字的堆叠，从上到下但从左到右阅读，使用红色小圆圈作为句号。第一列由顶部较大字体的标题组成：“穿越布鲁克林渡口”，后面是两列较小的字体，分别是“沃尔特·惠特曼”和“徐冰书法，一位曾居住在布鲁克林的艺术家，二千零八年”。在最后一列（右）的底部，艺术家的印章以红色显示。
title_original_en: Square Word Calligraphy: Crossing Brooklyn Ferry, Walt Whitman
description_original_en: The painting consists of two panels (a, b).  The first verse of Walt Whitman's "Crossing Brooklyn Ferry" written in "square word calligraphy," the hybrid writing form invented by the artist. This writing system consists of English words written with letters arranged in bunched stacks resembling Chinese characters, reading from top to bottom but left to right, using small red circles as periods.   The first column consists of the title in larger characters at the top: "Crossing Brooklyn Ferry," folowed by two columns of smaller characters reading "by Walt Whitman" and "Calligraphy by Xu Bing an artist who once lived in Brooklyn, two thousand and eightteen."  At the bottom of the last (right) column, the artist's seal appears in red.

title: 大马士革·阿坦，7岁
description: 穆罕默德·哈菲兹（Mohamad Hafez）的《大马士革阿坦7号》（Damascene Athan#7）是一件当代艺术作品，它与叙利亚和2011年开始的内战有着极好的视觉、听觉和概念联系。《大马士革阿丹》是12件系列作品的一部分，其标题与大马士革倭马亚大清真寺（Great Umayyad Mosque）的每日祈祷召唤相同，倭马亚清真寺是公元706年标志性的早期清真寺之一。虽然该系列中的每件作品都大不相同，但它们都被安排成大小相似的装饰镜架。在每一帧中，大马士革城市生活的不同场景都通过各种小型改造物品的突出组合反映出来，这些物品看起来像城市的建筑细节。这件作品在系列中编号为7，展示了一座有两扇门的建筑立面，其中一扇门位于一座历史悠久的拱门内。一辆代表叙利亚特勤局使用的丰田卡车停在外面。从大楼里伸出的监控摄像头让人们注意到叙利亚内战前夕平民每天过着恐惧和压力的生活。这件作品还包括艺术家在2011年最后一次访问该市时拍摄的录音。在音频中，可以听到倭马亚大清真寺的祈祷声，以及在街上玩耍的孩子们和包括艺术家在内的人们在咖啡馆用阿拉伯语交谈的声音。总的来说，这是一篇沉思的作品，反映了叙利亚的物理、历史和政治复杂性。
description_original_en: The Damascene Athan #7 by Mohamad Hafez is a contemporary art work that makes an excellent visual, audio, and conceptual link to Syria and the civil war that started in 2011.The Damascene Athan is part of a 12-piece series carrying the same title which derives from the daily call of prayers coming from the Great Umayyad Mosque in Damascus, one of the iconic early mosques dating to 706 CE. Although each piece in the series is quite different, they are all arranged as similar sized decorative mirror frames.  Within each frame a different vignette of city life in Damascus is reflected through the protruding ensemble of various small repurposed items made to look like the architectural details of the city.This piece, numbered 7 in the series, shows a building façade with two doors, one placed inside an historical arch. A Toyota truck representing those used by the Syrian secret service is parked outside.  A surveillance camera protruding from the building draws attention to the life of fear and stress lived daily by the civilians on the eve of the Syrian civil war.  The piece also includes an audio recording captured by the artist during his last visit to the city in 2011. In the audio, the call of prayer from the Great Umayyad Mosque as well as children playing in the streets and people including the artist talking in Arabic in a café can be heard. Overall it is a contemplative piece reflecting the physical, historical, and political complexities of Syria.

title: 文化负担篮子
description: 盘绕的篮子上装饰着来自不同部落的十一名妇女的身影，她们背着三维的负重篮和摇篮，采用部落的传统风格。每一种篮筐技术都体现在这件作品中，这是2015年圣达菲印度市场最佳表现奖的篮筐的复制品。
description_original_en: Coiled basket decorated with the figures of eleven women from different tribes carrying on their backs, three-dimensional burden baskets and cradles in the traditional style of their tribes. Every basketry technique is represented in the work, which is a duplicate of the basket that won Best of Show at the 2015 Santa Fe Indian Market.

title: 儿童衣架
description: 儿童大小的拟人化衣架，由一根方形钢制中心柱组成，白色粉末涂层，带有黑色点缀，顶部有一个程式化的朱鹭头，由一个加重的圆形底座支撑。一个装有几何心形的装饰性盒子表示形状的腹部。在大约肩部、腰部和臀部的高度，有突出的T形方形钢钉，上面有一组五个可拆卸的MDF腿和脚组件以及五个手和手臂组件，每个组件都有一个方孔，可以以各种配置排列。这是原型。
description_original_en: Child-sized anthropomorphic clothing rack composed of a center post of square steel, powder coated in white with black accents, topped with a stylized ibis head and supported by a weighted circular base.  A decorative box holding a geometric heart indicates the abdomen of the form. At approximately shoulder, waist, and hip height are protruding T-shaped square  steel pegs to which a set of five removable MDF leg and feet components and five hand and arm components, each with a square hole, that can be arranged in various configurations. This is the prototype.

These designs are directly inspired by William Morris wallpaper designs from the last quarter of the 19th century. Overlapping, off center, a green and red parakeet, a female Crimson Rosella, native to Australia.  Condition: some minor rubbing and scratches to outer rim front, gold shows marks from fingerprints. There is an inclusion in the thick gold painted area due to manufacture.

title: 结
description: 一个壮观的短视频“结”（3:45）显示了零碎的人物在各种建筑空间中漂浮、甩臀和跳舞。这部作品参考了巴洛克式建筑、嘻哈文化、黑人和LGBTQ亚文化，突出了欲望、资本主义痴迷和系统性压迫。纽瑟姆对艺术史和另类文化来源有着敏锐的认识，对当代文化和身份政治提供了有针对性的评论。
description_original_en: A spectacular short video “Knot” (3:45) shows fragmentary figures floating, twerking, and dancing in a variety of constructed spaces. The work references baroque architecture, hip-hop culture, and black and LGBTQ subcultures and highlights desire, capitalistic obsessions, and systemic oppressions. Newsome shows a keen awareness of art historical and alternative cultural sources to offer pointed commentaries on contemporary culture and identity politics.

title: Cervo椅（鹿椅）
description: 胡桃木胶合板，一种动物形状的椅子，由两个弯曲和切割的部分连接在一起，据设计师介绍，代表鹿的内脏。上背部由弯曲的、水平的、凹形的切口部分组成，切口部分朝前，有渐变大小的“骨头”，最小的“骨头“在靠背水平，座椅由相同的弯曲切口部分组成（垂直），切口部分与渐变大小的”骨头“相反，最小的”骨头”在靠背水平和前部，四个弯曲的“骨骼”在两侧形成椅子腿；两个椅子部分在反面用一块薄而平的模制胡桃木色塑料片连接在一起。
description_original_en: Walnut plywood, zoomorphic chair comprised of two bent and cut sections joined together to represent, according to the designer, the innards of a deer. Upper back formed with curved, horizontal, concave, cut out section facing forward, with graduating sized 'bones', the smallest 'bones' at back splat level, seat formed with same curved, cut out section inversed (vertical) with graduating sized 'bones', smallest 'bones' at back splat level and at front, four of the curved 'bones' form chair legs on either side; the two chair sections are attached together on the reverse with a thin, flat, molded, walnut colored plastic (?) sheet.

title: 最后的夕阳
description: 黄昏时分山峰的长方形绘画。左右两侧有两个艺术家的印章。艺术家的签名就在其中一个印章的正上方。没有其他铭文。关于这两幅画【2014.3.1，2014.3.2】，王曼生曾写道：我努力捕捉白天变成黑夜的瞬间。夜景在中国传统山水画中并不常见。但对我来说，正如诗人陶渊明（生于公元365年）在一首诗中所描述的那样，当空气清新，鸟儿回到栖息地时，山上光线减弱的那一刻是神奇的。王的绘画涉及中国古典主题和传统的手卷形式，但他使用了独特的材料。这些包括用煮核桃（外壳和肉）制成的自制墨水。然后，他在纸板上使用油墨和丙烯酸涂料，而不是传统的柔软吸水的手工纸。《戈壁之夜》描绘了沙丘吞没位于中国西北部敦煌的古代佛教洞穴教堂，这些教堂位于沙漠深处，沿着骆驼商队走过的丝绸之路。
description_original_en: Oblong painting of mountain peaks at twilight.  Two artist's seals on the right and left sides. Artist's signature on top right just above one of the seals. No other inscriptions.  About these two paintings [2014.3.1, 2014.3.2], Wang Mansheng has written:  I strove to capture the moment when daylight turns into night. Night scenes are uncommon in traditional Chinese landscape painting. But for me, that moment of waning light in the mountains, as the poet Tao Yuanming (born 365 C.E.) described it in a poem, when the air is fresh and the birds are returning to their roosts, is magical.  Wang's paintings refer to classical Chinese themes and the traditional handscroll format, but he uses unique materials. These include homemade ink made by boiling walnuts (husks and meat). He then uses the ink together with acrylic paint, on cardboard instead of the customary soft and absorbent handmade paper. Nightfall in the Gobi depicts the sand dunes engulfing the ancient Buddhist cave-chapels located at Dunhuang in northwestern China, deep in the desert along the paths that camel caravans traversed, known as the Silk Road.

月球冰月雕塑
description: 由顶部有裂纹的玻璃和底部形成的瓷器组成的不规则形状的块。这件作品似乎是一块发现的水晶，除了粘土基座一侧留下的印记，表明它是由一位陶艺家制作的。Ogawa Machiko是一位陶瓷艺术家，她经常将玻璃融入她的作品中。她的雕塑形式经常指冰与火的切割。这篇文章来自一个讨论她对地质学兴趣的小组。她开发了多种方法，将玻璃和粘土与各种矿物质和烧制条件结合起来，创造出新的效果。伴随着传统的木制储物箱，由艺术家签名并盖章。
description_original_en: Irregularly shaped block consisting of crackled glass at the top and formed porcelain at the bottom.  The piece appears to be a found piece of rock crystal except for the impressed finger marks on one side of the clay base, indicating that it was crafted by a ceramicist.  Ogawa Machiko is a ceramic artist who often incorporates glass into her work. Her sculptural forms often refer to the dicotomy of ice and fire. This piece is from a group that addresses her interest in geology. She has developed a variety of ways of combining glass and clay, together with various minerals and firing conditions, to create new effects.  Accompanied by the traditional wood storage box, signed and sealed by the artist.

description: 一个手工制作的花瓶，其开放式顶部呈不对称的波浪形图案，令人联想到插花的轮廓，沿着外缘用手工切割的垂直线条的瓷带进行装饰。颈部装饰有中央漩涡上方的切割垂直线，漩涡上有一朵花，周围是一个由小花蕾组成的卵形花环，两侧各有两片橡树叶。背部围绕颈部和两侧装饰有带有垂直切割线的带状装饰，也用于在中心制作一个普通的圆形漩涡。花瓶的卵形主体向底部逐渐变细，在一个张开的圆形底座上方，底座上装饰着均匀间隔的凹圆，上下都有徒手垂直线条的切割成型。底座中心有孔。状况：非常好。
description_original_en: A hand-built vase, the open top in an asymmetrical wavy pattern evocative of the silhouette of a flower arrangement, finished along the outer rim with an applied porcelain ribbon molding with incised freehand vertical lines. The neck decorated with incised vertical lines above a central cartouche with an applied flower surrounded by an ovoid garland of small applied buds, flanked either side by two applied oak leaves. The back decorated around the neck and sides with an applied ribbon molding with incised vertical lines, also used to create a plain circular cartouche at center. The ovoid body of the vase tapers towards the foot, above a flaring round base decorated with evenly spaced concave circles and finished above and below with an incised molding of freehand vertical lines. Hole in center of base.  CONDITION: Very good condition.

description: 这件作品是总部位于纽约市的建筑公司Diller Scofidio+Renfro和巧克力商Richard Capizzi的合作作品，由25个三维数字组成，用巧克力铸造，从最浅到最深按数字降序排列。随着数字变小，巧克力颜色变深，巧克力块的高度会降低。装在一个透明的塑料盒中（a=箱底和巧克力，b=盒盖），带有真空成型的巧克力盖。；盒子用纸包装。巧克力师：Richard Capizzi项目团队：Elizabeth Diller、Ricardo Scofidio、William Ngo、Alexandra E.Woolsey Puffer和Christopher Connock
description_original_en: A collaboration between New York City based architectural firm Diller Scofidio + Renfro and a chocolatier Richard Capizzi, the piece features twenty-five pieces of three-dimensional numbers, cast in chocolate and arranged in descending numerical order from lightest to darkest chocolate. As the numbers get smaller and the chocolate darker, the pieces decrease in height. Contained in a clear plastic box (a=box bottom & chocolates, b=box lid), with vaccuformed cover for chocolates.; The box wrapped in paper wrapper. Chocalatier:  Richard Capizzi Project Team: Elizabeth Diller, Ricardo Scofidio, William Ngo, Alexandra E. Woolsey Puffer & Christopher Connock

material_original_en:
Decoration in red, white; two greens, tan and gold on dark yellow ground. Five clawed dragon hunting jewel amid clouds. Lined. Selvage on two sides.

Decoration in red, white, 2 greens, tan and gold on dark yellow ground. 5 clawed dragon hunting jewel amid clouds, 1 in each of 4 corners and 1 in circular panel in center. All over pattern covering rest. Geometric line design of interlocking small hexagons and large 6 pointed stars. Stars surrounded by tangent circles. Conventional forms of juiheaded clouds tie these lines together and form central rosettes in stars. Lined. Selvage on 2 sides.

title: 宫女
description: 这张唐代宫女的照片保存得非常完好，可以欣赏到当时女性服装和妆容的细节。
description_original_en: This image of a Tang Dynasty court lady, exceptionally well preserved, allows to appreciate the details of the costume and makeup of the women of the time.

title: 有十二片叶子和一百只鸟的屏幕
description: 这个特殊的屏幕展示了安装在珍贵木材框架上的特殊性；每块面板的底部都雕刻着一只神话中的野兽，在花边云朵的背景下显得格外突出。
title_original_en: Screen with twelve leaves and a hundred birds
period_original_en: Dated 1725, Qing Dynasty, Reign of Yongzheng (1722-1735)
description_original_en: This exceptional screen presents the particularity of being mounted on a frame of precious wood; at the bottom of each of its panels is carved a mythical beast, standing out against a background of lacy clouds.

title: 玉碗，被称为“马扎林碗”
description: 这件杰出的作品，仿古和矫饰，是明代雕塑的见证。
description_original_en: This outstanding piece, archaising and mannerist, is a testimony of Ming sculpture.

title: “Richard Kan花瓶”
description: 这个特殊的中国花瓶，有着蓝白相间的装饰，可以追溯到14世纪中叶，是元代（1279-1368）在中国制作的一件作品，长期保存在一个法国家庭中。
description_original_en: This exceptional Chinese vase with its blue and white décor, dated to the mid-14thcentury, is a work crafted in China under the Yuan Dynasty (1279-1368), and long held in a French family.

title: 婚礼头饰
description: 被称为“凤凰头饰”的结婚头饰是汉族妇女在结婚时以及最正式的场合佩戴的，因此我们经常在祖先的肖像中看到它们。
description_original_en: The wedding headdress known as the “phoenix tiara” was donned by the women of the Han ethnic group on the occasion of their wedding, as well as for the most formal occasions –, thus we often find them represented in portraits of ancestors.

title: 波浪装饰碗
description: 这款波浪装饰碗的独创性归功于材料的精致和装饰的大胆图形主义。
description_original_en: The originality of this bowl with its wave decoration is owed to the delicacy of the material and the bold graphism of the décor.

title: 猪龙（朱龙）
description: 猪龙的字面意思是“猪龙”，它是一种神奇的生物，由一个蜿蜒的身体包裹在一个孔口上，末端是一个猪头，上面有两只竖起的耳朵、一双圆眼睛和一个扁平的皱巴巴的鼻子。
description_original_en: Literally meaning “pig-dragon”, thezhulongis a magical creature formed by a serpentine body wrapped around an orifice, and ending with a pig’s head topped by two raised ears, a pair of round eyes, and a flat wrinkled snout.

title: 女马球运动员
description: 中国古代的丧葬习俗对陵墓起着重要作用，是连接两种存在的纽带。因此，在装饰太平间时，要注意将木制或陶制的小雕像放在死者旁边，以确保他/她在遥远的地方有一个舒适的逗留。
description_original_en: The funerary practices of ancient China reserved an important role to the tomb, the link between two existences. Thus the care taken in decorating the mortuary chamber included placing figurines crafted in wood or terra-cotta beside the deceased to ensure him/her a comfortable sojourn in the beyond.
